**Assumptions:**
1. Steady, incompressible flow.
2. Inviscid actuator-disc theory.
3. Uniform velocity across the disc.
4. Tunnel cross-section equal to propeller disc.

**Derivation:** (as per Section 3.1)

Starting from momentum conservation across an ideal actuator disc:

$$
T = \dot{m}(V_b - V_a)
$$

With $\dot{m} = \rho A V_a$ and $V_b = 2V_a$:

$$
T = \rho A V_a^2
$$

Non-dimensionalising with $\rho n^2 D^4$:

$$
\boxed{T_0 = K_T \cdot \rho \cdot n^2 \cdot D^4}
$$

#### Model Solution to (b):

**Given:**
- $D = 2.5$ m
- $n = 300 \text{ rpm} = 5 \text{ rev/s}$
- $K_T = 0.42$
- $\eta_h = 0.80$
- $K_Q = 0.055$
- $\rho = 1025 \text{ kg/m}^3$

**(i) Open-water thrust:**

$$
\begin{aligned}
T_0 &= K_T \cdot \rho \cdot n^2 \cdot D^4 \\
&= 0.42 \times 1025 \times 25 \times (2.5)^4 \\
&= 0.42 \times 1025 \times 25 \times 39.0625 \\
&= 420{,}293 \text{ N} \\
&\approx 420.3 \text{ kN}
\end{aligned}
$$

**(ii) Effective side force:**

$$
\begin{aligned}
T_{eff} &= T_0 \cdot \eta_h \\
&= 420.3 \times 0.80 \\
&= 336.24 \text{ kN}
\end{aligned}
$$

**(iii) Power consumed:**

$$
\begin{aligned}
P &= 2\pi K_Q \rho n^3 D^5 \\
&= 2\pi \times 0.055 \times 1025 \times 125 \times (2.5)^5 \\
&= 0.3456 \times 1025 \times 125 \times 97.656 \\
&\approx 4.32 \times 10^6 \text{ W} \\
&\approx 4320 \text{ kW}
\end{aligned}
$$

---

### Question B (14 Marks) — Choice 2 (Internal Choice)
**[KTU University Exam – Sample Paper 2024, Module 4 Alternative]**

**(a)** With the help of a functional block diagram, explain the working of a **Voith-Schneider Propeller (VSP)** system. List two advantages and two disadvantages compared to a tunnel thruster. **(7 Marks)**

**(b)** A ship of length $L = 200$ m is fitted with a bow thruster producing an effective thrust $T_{eff} = 100$ kN at zero speed. If the resistance to sideways motion at zero speed is $R_{side} = 0.30 \cdot T_{eff}$, find: (i) Net side force accelerating the ship, (ii) Lateral acceleration of the ship if displacement $\nabla = 50{,}000$ tonnes, (iii) Comment on whether this is sufficient to perform a 90° turn in 5 minutes in calm water. **(7 Marks)**

#### Model Solution to (a):

**VSP Working Principle:**
1. A horizontal rotor carries 4–6 vertically-oriented blades around its periphery.
2. Each blade rotates about its **own vertical axis** (eccentric control lever).
3. By adjusting the eccentric position continuously, each blade's pitch changes during rotation, producing **thrust in any direction within the disc plane**.
4. The result: instantaneous 360° thrust vector without reversing rotation.

**Advantages over Tunnel Thruster:**
1. Instant 360° thrust direction (no clutch engagement lag).
2. Higher bollard-pull efficiency (~75% vs 70%).
3. Excellent dynamic-positioning capability.

**Disadvantages:**
1. Much higher capital cost (3–5× tunnel thruster).
2. Complex hydraulic pitch-control system → higher maintenance.

#### Model Solution to (b):

**Given:**
- $T_{eff} = 100$ kN $= 100{,}000$ N
- $R_{side} = 0.30 \times T_{eff} = 30{,}000$ N
- $\nabla = 50{,}000$ tonnes $= 50 \times 10^6$ kg

**(i) Net side force:**

$$
\begin{aligned}
F_{net} &= T_{eff} - R_{side} \\
&= 100{,}000 - 30{,}000 \\
&= 70{,}000 \text{ N} = 70 \text{ kN}
\end{aligned}
$$

**(ii) Lateral acceleration:**

$$
\begin{aligned}
a &= \frac{F_{net}}{m} = \frac{70{,}000}{50 \times 10^6} \\
&= 1.4 \times 10^{-3} \text{ m/s}^2
\end{aligned}
$$

**(iii) Lateral displacement in 5 minutes:**

$$
\begin{aligned}
s &= \frac{1}{2} a t^2 = \frac{1}{2} \times 1.4 \times 10^{-3} \times (300)^2 \\
&= 0.5 \times 1.4 \times 10^{-3} \times 90{,}000 \\
&= 63 \text{ m}
\end{aligned}
$$

Since the ship is 200 m long and must rotate 90°, a 63 m lateral shift is **marginally sufficient** but would require cooperative tug assistance for precise control.

---

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning — Common Pitfalls:**
> 1. **Unit conversion of rpm → rev/s** is missed by ~30% of students. Always write the conversion step explicitly.
> 2. **Forgetting the factor $\eta_h$** in $T_{eff}$ calculation — losing 2 marks.
> 3. **Wrong density**: KTU problems use either $1025$ (sea) or $1000$ (fresh). Do not hard-code.
> 4. **Power formula confusion**: Students mistakenly compute $P = T \cdot V$ (wrong — that's for translational power). For thrusters use $P = 2\pi n Q$.

---

## 5.3 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

- A **bow thruster** is a transverse propulsion unit in a tunnel below the bow waterline.
- The **fundamental thrust equation** is $T_0 = K_T \rho n^2 D^4$.
- The **effective thrust** at forward ship speed is $T_{eff} = \eta_h \cdot T_0$, with $\eta_h \approx 0.7$–$0.85$.
- The **power consumed** is $P = 2\pi K_Q \rho n^3 D^5$.
- **VSP** produces 360° thrust; **Azimuth thruster** combines propulsion + steering; **Tunnel thrusters** produce only sideways thrust.
- Bow thrusters are **ineffective above ~5 knots** due to asymmetric pressure and cavitation.
- Modern DP vessels combine **multiple azimuth + tunnel thrusters** for position keeping.
- Always **convert rpm → rev/s** before substituting in formulae.
- **Sea water density**: $1025$ kg/m³ (standard KTU value).
- Hull interaction factor accounts for **loss of effective side force** due to flow around the hull and tunnel geometry.
- For lateral acceleration problems, **ship mass** = displacement in kg ($1$ tonne = $1000$ kg).
